item.title: Federal Reserve holds interest rates steady amid resurgent inflation
item.description: Kevin Warsh's first Fed meeting as chairman of the central bank could offer clues about the path for interest rates this year.

item.title: Trump says Iran deal not final, will resume bombing "if they don't behave"
item.description: President Trump warns he could order new strikes if Iran's leaders "don't behave," two days before the expected signing of a deal that remains vaguely defined.

item.title: Here's why a gold investment makes sense right now
item.description: The benefits of a gold investment this June are multiple. Here's why it's especially worth investing in right now.

item.title: Tropical Storm Arthur forms off Texas Gulf Coast
item.description: Tropical Storm Arthur, the first named storm of the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season, formed Wednesday off the Gulf Coast of Texas.

item.title: Suspect in custody after deadly shooting at Wilmington Hospital
item.description: A suspect was taken into custody in Philadelphia in connection with the shooting at Wilmington Hospital that left a 19-year-old dead and another injured.

item.title: Read the 14 points of the agreement between Iran and the U.S.
item.description: The agreement, as read by senior U.S. officials, allows Iran to immediately begin exporting oil and petroleum products.

item.title: Fed votes to leave interest rates unchanged in first decision with Kevin Warsh as chair
item.description: The Federal Reserve board voted Wednesday to leave interest rates unchanged in its first decision since Kevin Warsh took over as chair. CBS News' Kelly O'Grady has more.

item.title: Trump meets with India's Modi at G7 as he navigates global interest in Iran deal
item.description: President Trump met with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi at the G7 summit, and emphasized that the two had a "very good" conversation despite past tensions between the two nations. CBS News' Ed O'Keefe has more context.

item.title: What the Fed rate pause could mean for mortgage interest rates now
item.description: The Fed held rates steady again on Wednesday. Here's what that continued pause could mean for mortgage borrowers.

item.title: Rex Heuermann sentenced to life in prison for Gilgo Beach serial killings
item.description: Rex Heuermann was sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole for the series of murders known as the Gilgo Beach serial killings.

item.title: The Obama Presidential Center and Library opens in Chicago
item.description: After more than a decade since it was announced, the Obama Presidential Center and Library officially opens to the public in Chicago's Jackson Park on June 19, 2026.

item.title: Gilgo Beach serial killer addresses court as he's sentenced to life in prison without parole
item.description: Rex Heuermann was sentenced to life in prison without parole Wednesday after pleading guilty to a series of murders known as the Gilgo Beach serial killings. Heuermann spoke at the hearing where the sentence was handed down. CBS News' Shanelle Kaul reports.

item.title: How the Strait of Hormuz blockade has forever changed the world's energy trade
item.description: President Trump said the Strait of Hormuz will reopen within "the next day or two." Semafor climate & energy editor Tim McDonnell explains why global energy trade will never be the same, even when the strait reopens.

item.title: Senate postpones Clayton's confirmation hearing after Trump upends plans
item.description: The Senate canceled Jay Clayton's confirmation hearing on Wednesday after President Trump's move to delay the installation of the new intelligence chief.

item.title: DOJ taps prosecutor to coordinate human trafficking, child exploitation cases
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche appointed Alessandra Serano to serve as the Justice Department's national coordinator on human trafficking and child exploitation cases.

item.title: Watch: Trump takes questions on Iran deal, Israel, Lebanon and more
item.description: President Trump held a news conference Wednesday at the G7 Summit in France, addressing a variety of issues. Following the president's remarks, Weijia Jiang, Nancy Cordes and Olivia Rinaldi provided context.

item.title: Victims ID'd in B-52 bomber crash that killed 8 at Edwards Air Force Base
item.description: As standard practice, the military makes public identifications 24 hours after next of kin have been notified.

item.title: Takeaways from Trump news conference on Iran deal
item.description: President Trump took questions Wednesday on the Iran deal, the situation in the Middle East and several other topics. CBS News' Weijia Jiang, Nancy Cordes and Olivia Rinaldi have more.

item.title: Georgia lawmakers not changing voting maps during special session
item.description: Georgia House leadership say they are holding off on redrawing Georgia's legislative maps​ during the special legislative session.

item.title: Rick Jackson spent $100 million in Georgia GOP governor primary to defeat Trump-backed opponent
item.description: Billionaire healthcare executive Rick Jackson defeated Trump-endorsed Lt. Gov. Burt Jones in Georgia's Republican gubernatorial runoff election, CBS News projects. Jackson will face Democrat Keisha Lance Bottoms in November. CBS News correspondent Skyler Henry has more.

item.title: Trump touts Iran agreement to wrap up G7 summit in France
item.description: President Trump defended the agreement reached by the U.S. and Iran​ at a press conference to close out the G7 summit in France.

item.title: Luigi Mangione's lawyers will argue psychiatric defense in NYS murder trial
item.description: Luigi Mangione's lawyers will argue that he was suffering from an extreme emotional disturbance when he allegedly killed UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson.

item.title: "No end in sight" as wind and rain batters South, Gulf Coast
item.description: Louisiana, Texas and Mississippi have been hit with near-constant rain over the past few days. Meteorologist Rob Marciano breaks down the forecast.

item.title: "The most famous judge in America, for a while," on highs and lows of a Trump trial
item.description: In an exclusive interview with CBS News, retired Justice Arthur Engoron reflected on the highs and lows of the 2023 Trump civil fraud trial.

item.title: What to expect as Kevin Warsh leads his first Fed interest rate meeting
item.description: New Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh is stepping in at a critical juncture for the U.S. economy, with inflation at its highest level in more than three years.

item.title: Social Security faces looming benefit cuts. Can the program be saved?
item.description: Social Security checks could see a 22% cut in 2032 unless Congress takes steps to shore up the program. Here are 5 ideas for fixing it.

item.title: JD Vance defends U.S-Iran deal as Trump expresses ire for Netanyahu at G7
item.description: "We want to tell the American people what's in this deal," Vice President JD Vance said on "CBS Mornings," adding that the public can expect to see the contents of the deal between Wednesday and Friday. Ed O'Keefe reports, and Imtiaz Tyab has more on President Trump's comments about Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.

item.title: Survivors of violence could see debt erased as N.Y. law goes into effect
item.description: A new law in New York makes it the eighth state to provide a path for coerced debt relief for survivors of domestic violence.

item.title: How much interest can a $50,000 CD account earn if opened now?
item.description: Interest earnings on a CD account of this size will be substantial. Here's what savers can expect to earn right now.

item.title: About Us
item.description: "60 Minutes" airs Sundays at 7 p.m. ET/PT
